ddls:
CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the setting */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the setting value (e.g., integer, boolean, string, date) */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the setting */,
  "is_required" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the setting is mandatory */,
  "is_overridable" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the setting can be overridden in child profiles */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the setting was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "profiles" (
  "profile_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each profile */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the profile */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the profile */,
  "parent_profile_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent profile (if applicable) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the profile is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the profile was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the profile */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the profile */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the profile */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("profile_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "profile_settings" (
  "profile_setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each profile setting */,
  "profile_id" INTEGER /* ID of the profile */,
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* ID of the setting */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the setting for the profile */,
  "is_inherited" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the setting is inherited from a parent profile */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the profile setting was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the profile setting */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the profile setting */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the profile setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("profile_setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_profile_settings_profile_id FOREIGN KEY ("profile_id") REFERENCES profiles ("profile_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_profile_settings_setting_id FOREIGN KEY ("setting_id") REFERENCES settings ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "setting_values" (
  "setting_value_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting value */,
  "profile_setting_id" INTEGER /* ID of the profile setting */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the setting at a given time */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the value was set */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the value is the default value */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the value is the current value */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the setting value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_value_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_setting_values_profile_setting_id FOREIGN KEY ("profile_setting_id") REFERENCES profile_settings ("profile_setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the user account */,
  "last_login_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* User account status (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_profiles" (
  "user_profile_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user profile */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "profile_id" INTEGER /* ID of the profile */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the profile is the default profile for the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user profile association was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the user profile association */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_profile_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "audit_log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "profile_id" INTEGER /* ID of the profile affected by the change */,
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* ID of the setting affected by the change */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Old value of the setting */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the setting */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the change was made */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_profile_id FOREIGN KEY ("profile_id") REFERENCES profiles ("profile_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_setting_id FOREIGN KEY ("setting_id") REFERENCES settings ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "profile_history" (
  "profile_history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each profile history */,
  "profile_id" INTEGER /* ID of the profile */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the profile was changed */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the changes made */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("profile_history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_profile_history_profile_id FOREIGN KEY ("profile_id") REFERENCES profiles ("profile_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_profile_history_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "profile_versions" (
  "profile_version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each profile version */,
  "profile_id" INTEGER /* ID of the profile */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the profile */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the version was created */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the changes in the version */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the version is the current version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("profile_version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_profile_versions_profile_id FOREIGN KEY ("profile_id") REFERENCES profiles ("profile_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "setting_versions" (
  "setting_version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting version */,
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* ID of the setting */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the setting */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the version was created */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the changes in the version */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the version is the current version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_setting_versions_setting_id FOREIGN KEY ("setting_id") REFERENCES settings ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user role association was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the user role association */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission" TEXT /* Permission granted to the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the permission was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)
